raw_step.reasoning: Notification guidance is time-sensitive and regulated, so it needed an official source check.

{"commands": ["python3 build_outputs.py", "python3 - <<'PY'
import os, zipfile
import build_outputs
build_outputs.build_outputs()
path='[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT]'
print('mtime', os.path.getmtime(path))
zf=zipfile.ZipFile(path)
for name in ['xl/worksheets/sheet5.xml','xl/worksheets/sheet8.xml']:
    data=zf.read(name).decode('utf-8')
    print(name)
    start=data.find('<row r="4">')
    print(data[start:start+500])

task: You are the incident commander for a metro carrier facing a dual fiber outage: a duct cut isolates a hospital district and a separate manhole fire degrades the alternate ring. Build an optimization-driven restoration sequence and an executive NOC incident-commander brief. Compute earliest restoration times under your proposed sequence, assign crews, allocate interim capacity across customers (including partial-service decisions where permitted), and estimate SLA-credit exposure. Include FCC/NOC notification triggers and a customer-tiered bridge update. Use the embedded incident packet (topology, cuts, crews, capacities, circuits, rules) and the provided standards references (FCC Part 4, ITU-T optical transport reliability, MEF SLA concepts). Deliver two artifacts: fiber_outage_restoration_optimizer.xlsx and noc_incident_commander_brief.md.
